title: Job Interview
definition: Key phrases and sample answers for common job interview questions.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.line: I have three years of experience in software development, specializing in web applications. I enjoy solving complex problems and working in teams.

dialogue.speaker: Interviewer
dialogue.line: Why do you want to work here?

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.line: I admire your company's focus on innovation, and I believe my skills align well with this role.

dialogue.speaker: You
dialogue.line: I'm a quick learner, and I communicate clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders.

practicePhrases: I have [X] years of experience in [field].
One of my key strengths is...
I'm looking for a role where I can grow and contribute.
In my previous role, I was responsible for...
I believe I would be a great fit because...
Do you have any questions for me?

commonMistakes.wrong: I am working here since 3 years.
commonMistakes.right: I have been working here for 3 years.
commonMistakes.why: Use Present Perfect Continuous with 'for + duration', not Present Continuous.

commonMistakes.wrong: My weakness is I am lazy sometimes.
commonMistakes.right: One area I'm working to improve is time management.
commonMistakes.why: Frame weaknesses constructively, focusing on improvement rather than a blunt negative trait.

quiz.question: Which is the best way to answer 'Tell me about yourself'?
options: Ask them to explain the question.
quiz.explanation: Interviewers expect a concise professional overview.

quiz.sentence: I am working in this company since 2020.
quiz.correction: I have been working in this company since 2020.
quiz.explanation: Use Present Perfect Continuous with 'since', not Present Continuous.
